description: Fetch readable web text and send webhook notifications.

tools.description:
Fetches a web page and returns its main readable text with navigation, ads, and other boilerplate stripped out.

When to use: Use when the user references a URL, asks to read or summarize an article or web page, or when an earlier step produced a link whose content you need to inspect. Do NOT use for binary downloads (PDFs, images) or for API endpoints — only for human-readable HTML pages.

How to communicate: Summarize the extracted text in plain prose; quote sparingly. If the response indicates `truncated:true`, tell the user the page was long and only the first portion was read.

Example: user says 'summarize https://example.com/article'. Call {url:'https://example.com/article'} -> read the returned text -> reply with a 3-bullet summary.

schema.name: url
schema.description: The full HTTP or HTTPS URL of the page to fetch. Must include the scheme. Example: `https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Oracle_Corporation` or `https://example.com/news/2026/article.html`.

_confDescriptions.timeout: HTTP timeout in seconds for the fetch request.
_confDescriptions.max_chars: Cap on extracted readable text length; returns truncated:true when hit.
_confDescriptions.max_bytes: Cap on raw response bytes downloaded; returns truncated:true when hit.
_confDescriptions.return_raw_html: If true, return raw HTML alongside extracted text. Default false.
_confDescriptions.user_agent: User-Agent header sent with the HTTP request. Default AIDP-Agent/1.0.

tools.description:
Sends a notification message to a Slack, Microsoft Teams, or generic HTTP webhook endpoint.

When to use: Use when the user asks to notify, alert, ping, message, or post to a channel/team/person — including human-in-the-loop pauses where the flow needs to tell someone it is waiting on input. Do NOT use for arbitrary REST API calls; this is for notification webhooks only.

How to discover inputs: The destination URL normally comes from the tool's `conf.webhook_url` set at deploy time. Only pass the `webhook_url` field when the user explicitly supplies a different URL at call time.

How to communicate: After sending, confirm in one sentence what was delivered and to which style (slack/teams/generic). Do not echo the webhook URL back to the user.

Example: user says 'tell the team the report is ready'. Call {title:'Report ready', message:'Q2 sales report is available in the workspace.'}.

schema.name: message
schema.description: The main body text of the notification. Plain text; Slack/Teams styles wrap it appropriately. Example: `Q2 sales report is ready in the workspace.` or `Flow paused — awaiting your approval on the deployment.`
examples: Q2 sales report is ready in the workspace.
Flow paused — awaiting your approval on the deployment.
Build #1423 failed: 2 tests broke in web_toolkit.
schema.default: Q2 sales report is ready in the workspace.

schema.name: title
schema.description: Optional short heading rendered above the message body (bolded in Slack/Teams). Keep under ~60 characters. Example: `Report ready` or `Deployment approval required`. Omit for a body-only notification.

schema.name: webhook_url
schema.description: Optional per-call override of the destination webhook URL. Only used when `conf.webhook_url` is empty — otherwise ignored. Format: full HTTPS URL issued by the Slack/Teams app or generic receiver. Example: `https://hooks.slack.com/services/T000/B000/XXXX`.

_confDescriptions.webhook_url: (secret) Incoming webhook URL — DO NOT commit. Provide via secret store at deploy time. Slack/Teams app config or generic endpoint.
_confDescriptions.style: Payload format: slack | teams | generic | generic_json. Selects how message/title are wrapped.
_confDescriptions.method: HTTP method used to deliver the webhook: POST (default) or PUT.
_confDescriptions.timeout: HTTP timeout in seconds for the webhook delivery.
